description: A design fails intermittently in the field because a clock-domain crossing passed functional simulation but was never CDC-verified.
body: Failure mode: nominal functional simulation never exposes metastability, so an unsynchronized crossing looks correct in the testbench while it occasionally fails on real silicon. Detection signals: passing simulation alongside intermittent hardware glitches, a structural CDC report flagging the crossing, and failures sensitive to temperature and voltage. Do not rely on functional simulation alone to clear clock-domain crossings. Safer fallback: run structural CDC analysis, add a proper synchronizer to the crossing, and inject metastability in simulation to confirm robustness.
